question_bank.Q01: Is the beneficiary's bank account linked to the scheme for DBT payments?
question_bank.Q02: Are the bank account number and IFSC code correct and up-to-date in the portal?
question_bank.Q03: Is the bank account currently active and operational (not frozen or dormant)?
question_bank.Q04: Has the NPCI Aadhaar mapper been verified for correct bank routing?
question_bank.Q05: Is there a duplicate beneficiary record under the same Aadhaar in the system?
question_bank.Q06: Has the Aadhaar number been seeded/linked to the scheme?
question_bank.Q07: Does the name and date of birth in Aadhaar match the scheme registration records?
question_bank.Q08: Is the Aadhaar number active and valid with UIDAI?
question_bank.Q09: Has eKYC or full KYC verification been completed for this scheme?
question_bank.Q10: Is a mobile number linked to the beneficiary's Aadhaar for OTP verification?
question_bank.Q11: Does the beneficiary's income fall within the scheme's eligibility limit?
question_bank.Q12: Are the land ownership records updated and matching in the Revenue department?
question_bank.Q13: Does the beneficiary meet the age requirement for this scheme?
question_bank.Q14: Is the beneficiary registered and enrolled in the scheme?
question_bank.Q15: Does the beneficiary fall under any exclusion criteria (government employee, income taxpayer)?
question_bank.Q16: Is the beneficiary's application currently under review or pending approval?
question_bank.Q17: Are there any data entry errors in the beneficiary's application or portal record?
question_bank.Q18: Is there any pending approval at the Block Development Officer (BDO) level?
question_bank.Q19: Is there any pending approval at the District Collector or district office level?
question_bank.Q20: Has the state portal successfully synced the beneficiary's data with the central system?
question_bank.Q21: Has the pensioner been incorrectly marked as deceased in any government record?
question_bank.Q22: Has the Digital Family Certificate (DFC) been submitted for the current year?
question_bank.Q23: Has the annual Jeevan Pramaan (life certificate) been submitted?
question_bank.Q24: Is the pension amount currently under revision or recalculation?
question_bank.Q25: Is there any dispute related to a co-pensioner or joint pension account?
question_bank.Q26: Is the ration card updated with all current family members?
question_bank.Q27: Has any diversion of PDS stock been reported against the FPS dealer?
question_bank.Q28: Has biometric authentication (fingerprint/iris) been failing at the Fair Price Shop?
question_bank.Q29: Has the monthly quota at the FPS dealer been exhausted before collection?
question_bank.Q30: Has the completed MGNREGA work been officially measured by the technical officer?
question_bank.Q31: Are there any errors or missing entries in the MGNREGA muster roll?
question_bank.Q32: Has the Fund Transfer Order (FTO) been generated by the Programme Officer?
question_bank.Q33: Was the MGNREGA wage payment rejected by the bank during processing?
question_bank.Q34: When was the last payment or benefit successfully received by the beneficiary?
question_bank.Q35: Has the beneficiary received any official SMS, letter, or portal notification about rejection?
question_bank.Q36: Is the beneficiary's registration number correct and traceable in the portal?
question_bank.Q37: Which specific government scheme is this grievance related to?
question_bank.Q38: How many months or installments is the payment overdue?
question_bank.Q39: Has the beneficiary visited the local CSC, block office, or Gram Panchayat recently?
question_bank.Q40: Is there an existing grievance or appeal already filed for this issue?
question_bank.Q41: Has the beneficiary's employment status changed (e.g., became a government employee)?
question_bank.Q42: Has the beneficiary recently migrated to a different district or state?
question_bank.Q43: Is the beneficiary's name on the PM-KISAN portal matching official documents?
question_bank.Q44: Has eKYC been completed on the PM-KISAN farmer portal?
question_bank.Q45: Is the MGNREGA job card active and does it have the correct details?
question_bank.Q46: Has the MGNREGA work been officially approved by the Gram Panchayat?
question_bank.Q47: Is DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) enabled for this beneficiary's scheme account?
question_bank.Q48: Has the bank passbook or account statement been checked for recent transactions?
question_bank.Q49: Is the disability certificate valid and currently active?
question_bank.Q50: Has the beneficiary's age, disability, or widow status been verified by a government officer?

causes.label: Bank Account Not Linked
causes.description: The beneficiary's bank account has not been linked to the scheme for DBT payments.

causes.label: Incorrect Bank Details
causes.description: The bank account number or IFSC code in the portal is incorrect or outdated.

causes.label: Bank Account Frozen or Dormant
causes.description: The beneficiary's bank account has been frozen or become dormant due to inactivity.

causes.label: NPCI Aadhaar Mapper Error
causes.description: The NPCI Aadhaar mapper is routing payments to an incorrect or closed bank account.

causes.label: Duplicate Beneficiary Record
causes.description: There is a duplicate beneficiary record under the same Aadhaar number in the system.

causes.label: DBT Not Enabled
causes.description: Direct Benefit Transfer has not been enabled for the beneficiary's scheme account.

causes.label: Aadhaar Not Seeded
causes.description: The beneficiary's Aadhaar number has not been seeded or linked to the scheme.

causes.label: Aadhaar Details Mismatch
causes.description: The name or date of birth in Aadhaar does not match the scheme registration records.

causes.label: Aadhaar Deactivated by UIDAI
causes.description: The beneficiary's Aadhaar number has been deactivated by UIDAI, causing payment failures.

causes.label: KYC Verification Pending
causes.description: eKYC or full KYC verification has not been completed for the scheme.

causes.label: Mobile Number Not Linked to Aadhaar
causes.description: No mobile number is linked to the beneficiary's Aadhaar, preventing OTP-based verification.

causes.label: Income Above Eligibility Threshold
causes.description: The beneficiary's income exceeds the scheme's eligibility limit.

causes.label: Land Records Mismatch
causes.description: Land ownership records in the Revenue department do not match the scheme portal records.

causes.label: Age Not Meeting Eligibility Criteria
causes.description: The beneficiary does not meet the minimum or maximum age requirement for the scheme.

causes.label: Beneficiary Not Registered in Scheme
causes.description: The beneficiary has not been enrolled or registered in the scheme.

causes.label: Beneficiary Falls Under Exclusion Criteria
causes.description: The beneficiary is excluded from the scheme as a government employee, income taxpayer, or other excluded category.

causes.label: Application Under Review or Pending Approval
causes.description: The beneficiary's application is still under review or awaiting approval in the system.

causes.label: Data Entry Error in Portal
causes.description: Incorrect data has been entered in the beneficiary's application or portal record.

causes.label: Approval Pending at Block Level
causes.description: The application or revision is awaiting approval from the Block Development Officer.

causes.label: Approval Pending at District Level
causes.description: The application or revision is awaiting approval from the District Collector or district office.

causes.label: State Portal Sync Error
causes.description: The state portal has not successfully synced the beneficiary's data with the central government system.

causes.label: Pensioner Incorrectly Marked as Deceased
causes.description: The pensioner has been erroneously marked as deceased in a government record, causing pension stoppage.

causes.label: Digital Family Certificate Not Submitted
causes.description: The Digital Family Certificate (DFC) has not been submitted for the current year.

causes.label: Annual Life Certificate Not Submitted
causes.description: The annual Jeevan Pramaan (life certificate) has not been submitted, causing pension suspension.

causes.label: Pension Amount Under Revision
causes.description: The pension amount is currently being revised or recalculated under a new government order.

causes.label: Co-Pensioner or Joint Pension Dispute
causes.description: There is a dispute or error related to a co-pensioner or joint pension account.

causes.label: Ration Card Not Updated
causes.description: The ration card has not been updated to include all current family members.

causes.label: FPS Dealer Diverting PDS Stock
causes.description: The Fair Price Shop dealer has been reported for diverting PDS stock meant for beneficiaries.

causes.label: Biometric Authentication Failure at FPS
causes.description: Biometric authentication (fingerprint or iris scan) is consistently failing at the Fair Price Shop.

causes.label: Monthly Quota Exhausted at FPS
causes.description: The monthly PDS quota at the Fair Price Shop has been exhausted before the beneficiary could collect.

causes.label: MGNREGA Work Not Measured
causes.description: The completed MGNREGA work has not been officially measured by the technical officer, blocking wage payment.

causes.label: Muster Roll Entry Error
causes.description: There are errors or missing entries in the MGNREGA muster roll affecting wage calculation.

causes.label: FTO Generation Pending
causes.description: The Fund Transfer Order (FTO) has not been generated by the Programme Officer, blocking wage disbursement.

causes.label: Wage Payment Rejected by Bank
causes.description: The MGNREGA wage payment was rejected by the bank during processing due to account or IFSC issues.

causes.label: System or Technical Error
causes.description: A technical or system error on the state or central portal is preventing benefit delivery.

resolutions.label: Link Bank Account to Scheme
steps: Collect the beneficiary's correct bank account number, IFSC code, and account holder name.
Log in to the scheme portal (e.g., pmkisan.gov.in) and navigate to the beneficiary's profile.
Enter and save the bank account details and verify via OTP or authorized officer confirmation.
Confirm that DBT is enabled and the account is successfully linked for future payments.
resolutions.authority: Lekhpal / BDO / CSC Operator

resolutions.label: Update Incorrect Bank Account Details
steps: Obtain the beneficiary's current bank passbook or account statement showing the correct account number and IFSC code.
Log in to the scheme portal and navigate to the beneficiary's bank details section.
Update the account number and IFSC code with the correct current details.
Submit the update and verify by checking the portal for payment processing in the next cycle.
resolutions.authority: BDO / CSC Operator / Scheme Nodal Officer

resolutions.label: Reactivate Frozen or Dormant Bank Account
steps: Advise the beneficiary to visit their bank branch with identity proof (Aadhaar) and passbook.
Request the bank to reactivate the dormant or frozen account by completing the KYC re-verification process.
Once reactivated, confirm the account status in the scheme portal.
Initiate a test transaction or wait for the next payment cycle to confirm successful credit.
resolutions.authority: Bank Branch Manager / BDO

resolutions.label: Fix NPCI Aadhaar Mapper Routing Error
steps: Verify the current NPCI Aadhaar mapper status by checking the Aadhaar seeding details on the scheme portal.
Request the beneficiary to visit their bank and submit a request to update the NPCI Aadhaar mapper to the correct bank account.
Follow up with the bank to confirm the NPCI mapper update has been processed.
Verify the correction on the NPCI portal and confirm the next payment is routed correctly.
resolutions.authority: Bank Branch / NPCI Nodal Officer / BDO

resolutions.label: Resolve Duplicate Beneficiary Record
steps: Search the scheme portal for all records associated with the beneficiary's Aadhaar number.
Identify the duplicate records and determine which record is the authentic and active one.
Raise a deduplication request with the scheme's nodal officer or state NIC team to merge or delete the duplicate.
Confirm that only one active record remains and that payments resume to the correct account.
resolutions.authority: State NIC / Scheme Nodal Officer / BDO

resolutions.label: Enable Direct Benefit Transfer for Scheme Account
steps: Verify the beneficiary's bank account is linked and active on the scheme portal.
Navigate to the DBT settings section of the beneficiary's portal profile.
Enable DBT for the scheme and confirm the Aadhaar-bank seeding is complete.
Check the next payment cycle to ensure the DBT transfer is successfully processed.
resolutions.authority: BDO / CSC Operator / Scheme Nodal Officer

resolutions.label: Seed Aadhaar Number to Scheme
steps: Collect the beneficiary's Aadhaar card and verify the number is active with UIDAI.
Log in to the scheme portal and navigate to the beneficiary's Aadhaar seeding section.
Enter the Aadhaar number and complete eKYC verification using OTP or biometric authentication.
Confirm the seeding is successful and that the beneficiary profile shows Aadhaar as linked.
resolutions.authority: CSC Operator / BDO / Lekhpal

resolutions.label: Correct Aadhaar Name or DOB Mismatch
steps: Collect official documents showing the correct name and date of birth (voter ID, birth certificate, school certificate).
If the Aadhaar record is incorrect, guide the beneficiary to update it at an Aadhaar Seva Kendra.
If the scheme record is incorrect, raise a data correction request with the BDO or scheme nodal officer.
Once correction is made, re-verify eKYC and confirm the beneficiary record is updated.
resolutions.authority: BDO / Scheme Nodal Officer / Aadhaar Seva Kendra

resolutions.label: Reactivate Deactivated Aadhaar with UIDAI
steps: Guide the beneficiary to visit the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra or enrolment centre.
Submit a reactivation request to UIDAI with supporting identity and address proofs.
Track the UIDAI reactivation status using the EID or acknowledgement number.
Once Aadhaar is reactivated, re-seed it on the scheme portal and verify NPCI mapper update.
resolutions.authority: UIDAI / Aadhaar Seva Kendra / BDO

resolutions.label: Complete eKYC or Full KYC Verification
steps: Identify which KYC step is pending (eKYC, full KYC, or PM-KISAN portal-specific KYC).
Guide the beneficiary to the nearest CSC or BDO office to complete the KYC process.
Complete eKYC using Aadhaar OTP or biometric authentication on the scheme portal.
Confirm KYC status is updated to verified on the scheme portal and notify the beneficiary.
resolutions.authority: CSC Operator / BDO / Scheme Nodal Officer

resolutions.label: Link Mobile Number to Aadhaar
steps: Advise the beneficiary to visit the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra or enrolment centre.
Submit a mobile number update request with the beneficiary's biometric verification.
Once the mobile number is linked (usually within 5–10 days), complete OTP-based eKYC on the scheme portal.
Confirm the mobile linkage is reflected on the UIDAI portal and scheme records.
resolutions.authority: UIDAI / Aadhaar Seva Kendra / CSC Operator

resolutions.label: Verify and Reassess Income Eligibility
steps: Collect the beneficiary's income certificate issued by the Revenue Department or Tehsildar.
Compare the stated income against the scheme's income eligibility threshold.
If eligible, raise a correction request with the BDO or scheme nodal officer to update the portal status.
If ineligible, inform the beneficiary of the reason and the appeals process available.
resolutions.authority: Revenue Department / Tehsildar / BDO

resolutions.label: Update and Reconcile Land Records
steps: Obtain the latest land ownership records (Khatauni/Khasra) from the Patwari or Revenue office.
Compare the Revenue records with the data on the scheme portal and identify discrepancies.
Raise a land record correction request with the Patwari office and update the scheme portal accordingly.
After update, re-verify eligibility on PM-KISAN portal and confirm payment resumption.
resolutions.authority: Patwari / Lekhpal / Revenue Department / BDO

resolutions.label: Verify Age Eligibility for Scheme
steps: Collect the beneficiary's age proof documents (Aadhaar, birth certificate, voter ID).
Verify the age against the scheme's eligibility criteria (minimum/maximum age).
If eligible, raise a data correction request to update the age in the portal.
If ineligible, explain the criteria to the beneficiary and suggest alternative applicable schemes.
resolutions.authority: BDO / Scheme Nodal Officer / Tehsildar

resolutions.label: Register Beneficiary in the Scheme
steps: Collect all required documents (Aadhaar, bank passbook, land records, income certificate as applicable).
Visit the nearest CSC, BDO office, or Gram Panchayat to initiate the registration.
Complete the online registration form on the scheme portal with all required details.
Follow up on the application status and confirm approval within the expected processing time.
resolutions.authority: CSC Operator / BDO / Gram Panchayat

resolutions.label: Review and Address Scheme Exclusion
steps: Review the specific exclusion criteria applicable to the scheme (government employee, income taxpayer, etc.).
Collect documents disproving the exclusion if the beneficiary believes the exclusion is erroneous.
Raise an appeal or grievance with the scheme nodal officer providing supporting documents.
If exclusion is valid, inform the beneficiary and guide them to other applicable welfare schemes.
resolutions.authority: Scheme Nodal Officer / BDO / District Collector

resolutions.label: Escalate Pending Application Review
steps: Check the current application status on the scheme portal and note the pending stage.
Contact the BDO or scheme nodal officer to request expedited review of the application.
If unresolved within the stipulated time, file a formal grievance on the scheme's grievance portal.
Follow up regularly and escalate to the district level if not resolved within expected timelines.
resolutions.authority: BDO / Scheme Nodal Officer / District Collector

resolutions.label: Correct Data Entry Errors in Portal
steps: Identify the specific field(s) with incorrect data by comparing portal records with original documents.
Collect supporting documents proving the correct information (Aadhaar, bank passbook, land records).
Submit a data correction request to the BDO or CSC operator with the supporting documents.
Verify the correction on the portal and confirm that the beneficiary's record is accurate.
resolutions.authority: CSC Operator / BDO / Scheme Nodal Officer

resolutions.label: Follow Up with Block Office for Pending Approval
steps: Visit the BDO office with the application reference number and all supporting documents.
Request the status of the pending approval and the expected clearance date.
If waiting beyond the stipulated time, submit a written complaint to the BDO.
Escalate to the District Collector's office if the block-level approval remains pending beyond 30 days.
resolutions.authority: BDO / Block Nodal Officer

resolutions.label: Follow Up with District Office for Pending Approval
steps: Visit the District Collector's office or district scheme office with the application reference number.
Request the status of the pending approval and identify the specific officer responsible.
Submit a written follow-up request with the supporting documents and application history.
If unresolved beyond 30 days, escalate to the state-level scheme directorate or file a RTI.
resolutions.authority: District Collector / District Nodal Officer

resolutions.label: Resolve State Portal Synchronization Error
steps: Verify the beneficiary's data entry on the state portal and confirm it is complete and correct.
Check the last sync status and identify any error codes on the state portal.
Raise a technical support ticket with the state NIC or IT department for the sync issue.
Follow up with the state scheme directorate to confirm the data has synced with the central system.
resolutions.authority: State NIC / State Scheme Directorate / IT Department

resolutions.label: Restore Live Status for Pensioner Marked Deceased
steps: Obtain a life certificate or Jeevan Pramaan issued by an authorized government officer.
Collect additional documents: Aadhaar, voter ID, and affidavit affirming the pensioner is alive.
Submit the documents to the pension disbursing authority and the District Social Welfare Officer.
Follow up to confirm the deceased flag is removed and pension payments are resumed with arrears.
resolutions.authority: District Social Welfare Officer / Pension Disbursing Authority / BDO

resolutions.label: Submit Digital Family Certificate
steps: Guide the beneficiary or family to submit the Digital Family Certificate (DFC) online or at the BDO office.
Ensure all required family member details and supporting documents are included.
Submit the DFC on the pension portal or at the pension disbursing authority's office.
Confirm DFC acceptance and check that pension payments resume in the next cycle.
resolutions.authority: BDO / Pension Disbursing Authority

resolutions.label: Submit Annual Life Certificate (Jeevan Pramaan)
steps: Advise the pensioner to generate a Jeevan Pramaan at the nearest CSC, BDO office, or bank branch.
Use biometric verification (fingerprint) to generate the digital life certificate.
Submit the Jeevan Pramaan ID to the pension disbursing authority or upload on the pension portal.
Confirm that the life certificate has been accepted and pension payments will resume in the next cycle.
resolutions.authority: CSC Operator / Bank Branch / BDO / Pension Disbursing Authority

resolutions.label: Clarify and Resolve Pension Amount Revision
steps: Obtain the official government order or notification regarding the pension revision.
Visit the District Social Welfare Office or pension disbursing authority to understand the new amount.
If the revision is incorrect or delayed, submit a representation to the District Collector.
Confirm in writing the revised amount and the effective date, and ensure arrears are paid if applicable.
resolutions.authority: District Social Welfare Officer / Pension Disbursing Authority / District Collector

resolutions.label: Resolve Co-Pensioner or Joint Pension Dispute
steps: Collect documents establishing the beneficiary's claim (marriage certificate, legal heir documents).
Visit the pension disbursing authority to report the co-pensioner dispute with supporting documents.
Request a formal inquiry by the District Social Welfare Officer to adjudicate the dispute.
Follow up to ensure the correct beneficiary's pension is restored and any wrongful payments are recovered.
resolutions.authority: District Social Welfare Officer / Pension Disbursing Authority / District Collector

resolutions.label: Update Ration Card with Correct Family Members
steps: Collect documents for all family members to be added (birth certificates, Aadhaar cards).
Visit the Food Supply Office or submit an online application on the state food portal.
Submit the update request with supporting documents and receive an acknowledgement.
Follow up to confirm the ration card is updated and the new members can collect their entitlement.
resolutions.authority: Food Supply Officer / Block Supply Officer / District Food Controller

resolutions.label: Report and Act on FPS Dealer Diversion
steps: Document the evidence of diversion (purchase records, witness statements, shortage dates).
File a formal complaint with the Block Supply Officer and District Food Controller.
Report the diversion on the state food department's grievance portal or toll-free helpline.
Follow up to ensure an inquiry is conducted and appropriate action is taken against the dealer.
resolutions.authority: Block Supply Officer / District Food Controller / State Food Department

resolutions.label: Resolve Biometric Authentication Failure at FPS
steps: Verify whether the issue is with the beneficiary's Aadhaar biometric or the FPS dealer's device.
If Aadhaar biometric issue, guide the beneficiary to update biometrics at an Aadhaar Seva Kendra.
If device issue, report to the Block Supply Officer to inspect and repair or replace the PoS device.
As interim relief, request the use of OTP-based authentication if biometric is temporarily unavailable.
resolutions.authority: Block Supply Officer / UIDAI / FPS Dealer Supervisor

resolutions.label: Report Monthly Quota Shortage at FPS
steps: Document the dates the beneficiary visited the FPS and was denied due to quota exhaustion.
File a complaint with the Block Supply Officer with the FPS dealer's code and specific shortage details.
Request allocation of additional quota or transfer of entitlement to another FPS temporarily.
Follow up with the District Food Controller to ensure the shortage is investigated and not recurrent.
resolutions.authority: Block Supply Officer / District Food Controller / State Food Department

resolutions.label: Request Official Measurement of Completed MGNREGA Work
steps: Collect the muster roll records and Gram Panchayat certification of work completion.
Submit a written request to the BDO or Programme Officer for urgent technical measurement.
Follow up with the Junior Engineer or Technical Officer responsible for work measurement.
Once measured, confirm the measurement report is submitted to enable FTO generation.
resolutions.authority: Junior Engineer / Programme Officer / BDO

resolutions.label: Correct Errors in MGNREGA Muster Roll
steps: Obtain a copy of the current muster roll and identify the missing or incorrect attendance entries.
Collect evidence of actual attendance (witnesses, photographs, Gram Panchayat records).
Submit a correction request to the Gram Rozgar Sahayak (GRS) or Programme Officer with evidence.
Confirm the corrected muster roll is updated on the NREGASoft portal and initiate revised FTO.
resolutions.authority: Gram Rozgar Sahayak / Programme Officer / BDO

resolutions.label: Escalate Pending FTO Generation
steps: Verify that work measurement and muster roll are complete on the NREGASoft portal.
Submit a written request to the Programme Officer for immediate FTO generation.
If not resolved within 7 days of due date, escalate to the BDO with documentation of the delay.
File a grievance on the MGNREGA grievance portal if wages are delayed beyond the statutory 15-day period.
resolutions.authority: Programme Officer / BDO / District Programme Coordinator

resolutions.label: Resolve Bank Rejection of MGNREGA Wages
steps: Obtain the bank rejection notice or check the NREGASoft payment status for the rejection reason.
Update the correct bank account number and IFSC code in the NREGASoft portal.
Initiate a re-generation of the FTO with the corrected bank details.
Confirm with the bank that the revised FTO has been processed and wages credited.
resolutions.authority: Programme Officer / BDO / Bank Branch

resolutions.label: Report and Resolve System Technical Error
steps: Document the technical error with screenshots, error codes, and the affected beneficiary details.
Report the issue to the state NIC help desk or the scheme's technical support portal.
Escalate to the District Informatics Officer (DIO) if not resolved within 5 working days.
Follow up to confirm the technical issue is resolved and the beneficiary's benefits are disbursed.
resolutions.authority: State NIC / District Informatics Officer / Scheme IT Helpdesk
